01
Case Study 01 · Luxury Vegan Accessories · USA
Execuluxe

A Florida-based luxury brand reinventing the professional woman's travel bag — vegan leather rollerbriefs that convert from a structured tote to a wheeled carry-on. Priced at $669–$699, these are high-consideration, high-AOV products that demand precision targeting and persuasive creative to convert.

Campaign Platform
Facebook & Instagram (Meta)
Campaign Duration
30 Days — December 2023
Product Price Point
$669 – $699 per Rollerbrief
Target Market
Professional Women, USA, Ages 28–52
Total Ad Spend
$1,500
30-day campaign
Total Revenue
$9,366
Direct attributable sales
ROAS
6.24×
Return on ad spend
Units Sold
14
Rollerbriefs purchased
Cost Per Sale
$107
On a $684 avg. order
The Challenge

Converting cold traffic on a $669 product

Selling a near-$700 bag to a stranger on social media is not simple. The product is exceptional — but without the right creative, the right audience, and the right message, budget disappears fast with nothing to show for it.

Execuluxe had a stunning product and genuine customer love, but no structured paid media presence. Zero consistent ad spend. No retargeting infrastructure. No video creative built for Meta's algorithm.

They needed someone to build it from scratch and make it profitable from day one.

Our Strategy

Product-led storytelling + layered targeting

  • Video-first creative showing the bag's signature transformation — tote to rollerbrief in seconds — designed to stop the scroll within the first 2 seconds
  • Hook copywriting that led with the professional woman's pain points: heavy bags, messy travel, sacrificing style for function
  • Layered audience targeting combining interest stacks (executive women, business travel, luxury fashion) with lookalike audiences built from website visitors
  • Retargeting funnel re-engaging video viewers and product page visitors with testimonial-led creatives and urgency-based offers
  • Q4 timing capitalised on peak gifting season — women buying for themselves and partners buying as gifts
Campaign Architecture — 3-Phase Funnel
01
Awareness — Cold Traffic
Short-form Reels and video ads targeting broad interest audiences of professional women in the USA. Goal: get the product seen. Metric tracked: video views, CPM, 3-second view rate.
02
Consideration — Warm Audiences
Retargeted video viewers (50%+ watch) and website visitors with social proof creatives — customer reviews, unboxing moments, lifestyle shots. Pushed them to product pages.
03
Conversion — Hot Audiences
Tight retargeting of cart abandoners and product page visitors. Urgency messaging — "Holiday shipping deadline", "Free accessories when you order today". Direct checkout CTAs.

Total Ad Spend
"
"Every dollar in ad spend had to justify itself — when your product costs $669, you can't afford to guess. We built a campaign architecture that matched the product's luxury positioning, and the ROAS proved it: 6× returns in 30 days is not luck, it's structure."
— Echobroad Agency · Campaign Strategy Notes, Execuluxe Campaign
02
Case Study 02 · Artisan Fragrance · USA
Elysian Parfum

Atlanta-based award-winning indie fragrance house founded by perfumer Jessi Park in 2021. Every bottle is handcrafted in small batches using fine naturals. Winner of Best New Fragrance 2024 (New You Beauty Awards) for Lavender Milk Tea. Products range from $32 discovery sets to $120+ full bottles — a unique AOV challenge requiring a multi-offer ad strategy.

Campaign Platform
Facebook & Instagram (Meta)
Campaign Duration
30 Days — December 2023
Product Price Point
$32 Discovery Sets → $120 Full Bottles
Target Market
Fragrance Enthusiasts, USA, Ages 22–45
Total Ad Spend
$1,200
30-day campaign
Total Revenue
$6,480
Direct attributable sales
ROAS
5.4×
Return on ad spend
Units Sold
78
Bottles & discovery sets
Avg Order Value
$83
Per transaction
The Challenge

Scaling an artisan brand without losing its soul

Elysian Parfum had built genuine product love and a small but loyal fanbase through organic content. The fragrances were award-winning — but the brand's reach was limited to people who stumbled onto them.

The challenge: fragrance is impossible to demo online. You can't smell an ad. Conversion depends entirely on emotional storytelling, social proof, and the power of your creative to make someone feel the scent before they buy it.

Additionally, the product range spanned from a low-entry discovery set ($32) up to full bottles ($120+), requiring different creative and CTA strategies per price point.

Our Strategy

Sensory storytelling meets data-driven targeting

  • Sensory-evocative video creative — close-up shots of bottles, liquid, application, atmospheric lighting. If you can't smell it, make them feel it visually
  • Discovery Set as the lead offer — low-risk entry point ($32) drove first-time buyers, with retargeting pushing full-bottle upsells
  • Award angle — "Best New Fragrance 2024" headline hook grabbed instant credibility in the first 2 seconds of every ad
  • Review-led social proof — customer testimonials embedded in ad copy; scent descriptions written in visceral, poetic language matching the brand's voice
  • Fragrance interest + gifting audience targeting capitalised on Q4 gift-buying season
Campaign Architecture — 3-Phase Funnel
01
Hook — Lead with the Award
Cold traffic ads led with "Best New Fragrance 2024" — instant credibility for an indie brand. Targeting: perfume lovers, niche fragrance communities, luxury lifestyle interests, ages 22–45 USA.
02
Entry Offer — Discovery Set
The $32 discovery set was the conversion engine. Low barrier to entry. Comes with $55 off a full bottle — self-funding the next purchase. Retargeting pushed full-bottle buyers who sampled first.
03
Scale — Upsell Full Bottles
Discovery purchasers were retargeted with full-bottle creatives. Copy acknowledged their trial purchase and pushed the upgrade. Result: LTV multiplied beyond the initial ad conversion.

Why It Worked
Award Credibility Hook
"Best New Fragrance 2024" stopped the scroll instantly
Low-Risk Entry Offer
$32 discovery set removed purchase hesitation
Upsell Funnel
$55 coupon inside discovery sets drove full-bottle repeats
"
"You cannot sell scent on social media — you can only sell a feeling. Every creative we built for Elysian Parfum was designed to make someone feel the fragrance before they could smell it. That emotional bridge is what converted 78 units in 30 days on a $1,200 budget."
— Echobroad Agency · Campaign Strategy Notes, Elysian Parfum Campaign
